description: Critical business rules governing Lakeshore Development operations. These rules are derived from CEO directives, operational interviews, and documented processes.

cpm_gates.description: Critical Path Method gates enforcing construction phase sequence. No phase can begin until the previous phase's inspections are all passed.

phases.name: Pre-Construction / Foundation
phases.draw_dependency: 1st draw unlocks up to Robintec; 2nd draw unlocks rest of Phase 1

phases.name: Rough / Structure
phases.key_bottleneck: Frame Inspection (requires ALL roughs + drain + doors/windows)

phases.name: Finish / Acabamento
phases.key_bottleneck: Shower Glass Door (depends on tile + vinyl completion)

phases.name: Closing / Warranty
phases.key_bottleneck: Impact fees blocking Final Electric (Marion County)

cpm_gates.rule: STRICT_SEQUENCE — no phase can begin until ALL gate inspections of the previous phase are passed

payment_triggers.description: Rules governing when subcontractor payments are authorized

rules.detail: When a monitor/supervisor marks a service as 'checklist done' in Builder Trend, this automatically triggers the payment process for the associated subcontractor.
rules.risk: Sub paid for incomplete work will NOT return to fix issues

rules.rule: Leo approval required for ALL payments
rules.detail: No payment can be executed without Leo's (Leonardo) explicit approval. This is the final gate regardless of amount.
rules.risk: Bottleneck if Leo is unavailable; payments queued

rules.detail: Every service should have a Purchase Order created before the sub executes work. Invoices without POs require emergency PM approval (Moises/Samuel).
rules.risk: Supervisors frequently bypass this rule, causing financial tracking issues

permit_rules.description: Rules governing building permits across counties

rules.detail: Building permits expire 180 days after issuance if construction has not started. In Marion County, no warning email is sent before expiration.
rules.risk: Expired permit = restart entire application process

rules.rule: Survey is prerequisite for all permit documents
rules.detail: Survey must be received before any other permit document can be prepared (energy calculations, trusses, floor plan submission).
rules.risk: Survey delays cascade to entire permit pipeline

rules.rule: Citrus County requires waivers and arrows
rules.detail: Every house in Citrus County requires additional waiver and arrow documentation. Recurrent rework if not anticipated.

rules.rule: Builders Risk insurance required before construction
rules.detail: Builders Risk insurance must be active and NOC (Notice of Commencement) filed before construction can legally begin.

material_adjustment.description: Mauricio's requirement for material change notification
material_adjustment.rule: Material changes by Compras must be communicated to Scheduling with sufficient lead time
material_adjustment.detail: When Purchasing changes a material specification (different vendor, different product), Scheduling must be notified with enough lead time to reschedule affected services. Last-minute notifications cause sub cancellations and additional costs.

rules.rule: Budget is an ESTIMATE, not a cap
rules.detail: Lakeshore uses cost-plus pricing. The budget presented to the client is an estimate (R3). Actual costs (Real) may differ. Variations are the client's exposure.
Lakeshore does NOT inflate prices to cover risk. Client pays actual execution cost + Lakeshore margin. This requires rigorous cost tracking.

rules.rule: Change orders pass through to client
rules.detail: Material price fluctuations and change orders are documented and passed through to the client with full transparency.
Every operational and technology decision is evaluated by Return on Investment. Tools that reduce cycle time are prioritized over tools that reduce headcount.

cycle_time_kpis.description: Target construction cycle times by market/product type

targets.notes: Standard spec homes in Marion County
Community homes, slightly longer due to logistics
Custom homes with client change cycles

cycle_time_kpis.measurement: Days from permit issuance to Certificate of Occupancy

draw_structure.description: Financial draw structure for construction financing
draw_structure.notes: BT does not support partial draw payments. Partial payments tracked manually in spreadsheets.

contractor_splits.description: Payment split structures for major subcontractors

contractors.name: FOCO
contractors.detail: 40% at start, 40% at rough completion, 20% at final

contractors.name: ECOWAN
contractors.detail: 40% at start, 30% at rough completion, 30% at final

contractor_splits.notes: Exact values change with volume. No up-to-date contracts with fixed values for all 27+ models. FIN-03 pain point.

rules.rule: Warranty starts at date of occupancy/sale
rules.detail: Company warranty period begins when the house is occupied or sold, NOT at CO issuance date.
Subcontractor warranty is 1 year from end of their service, which may differ from company warranty start. Creates conflict when sub's warranty expires before company's.
rules.risk: Repair costs fall on Lakeshore if sub warranty expired but company warranty still active

concessionaire_model.description: Expansion strategy for scaling through concessionaires
